IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
This work is taking place near Blackwall
Road. A road closure will be in place to allow
the works to be carried out safely. Access to
your property will always be provided.
To provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, we carry out regular inspections of trees
near our overhead cables. This has identified trees that have grown close, which could damage
the cables and affect your electricity supply.
Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely cut the trees away from
2 sections of cables. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the
future as we appreciate some customers may have experienced unplanned power cuts in the last
6 months.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
This work is taking place near Watling Street.
We are connecting a new property to the underground electricity network, following a request from
a customer. Due to the type of electricity cables we need to make the connecting to our engineers
will need to temporarily switch off your power so they can safely carry out this work.
Once completed this will adhere to the customer request as we have a duty to provide electricity
when requested. This will also ensure the network is working to the best of its ability. We
appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months and we
apologise for this.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
This work is taking place near Browick Road.
We regularly inspect our electrical equipment to ensure it is safe and efficient. We need to examine
a transformer on our electricity poles to check it is functioning properly. This essential work helps
to provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, reducing the risk of any unplanned power
cuts in the future as we appreciate some customers may have experienced unplanned power cuts
in the last 6 months.To keep everyone safe while we carry out these works, we will need to temporarily turn off your
electricity. We are taking extra measures to limit the time your power is off and we'll do our best to
cause as little disruption as possible. Doing this will improve the reliability of the electricity network
in the future.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: Following from a request from a third party, we are diverting an overhead electricity cable to allow for a new development to be connected to the network. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut, as we appreciate some customers may have experienced unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months.Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely carry out the work. Doing this will ensure our network is safe whilst works commence in the area, whilst giving the customer the desired outcome. During this time, you will be connected to a generator to provide you with electricity whilst we connect the new equipment and complete the work safely. To connect and disconnect the generator we will need to switch off your power temporarily.This work is taking place at Church Road.

Steps.Message:
Hi, Theo from the messaging team here. Our engineers have been in touch to let us know that they have been able to access the majority of the affected properties to carry out safety checks on our equipment, and Thames Water have allowed us to restore power to our electricity network following the flooding.

We understand that some houses may still be without power pending repairs of their internal wiring and equipment - This will be completed by the property owners or building management.

We know that being without power can be difficult, and we're sorry for the length of time you were without your electricity.

If you need any guidance or if you have any questions, please call us on 08003163105, which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Hi there. We've just had a shift change, so David, Decasta, Roland and Aaron are now here to keep you updated with progress from our field teams.

We know it's frustrating being without power, but we wanted to assure you we're still here and as soon as we have some more information we'll update you again.

Steps.Message:
Hi, it's Adam from the messaging team at UK Power Networks. I understand how difficult it can be without power and the additional worries this can bring.

For both our business customers and those at home, we will work as quickly and safely as possible to restore supplies. If you need any additional support or have any concerns, please call us on 105 or tweet us @ukpowernetworks.

I will personally continue to keep you updated with our progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 16:57 - Thanks for your patience over today.

Our engineers have let us know that excavations are continuing to expose more of our underground network for the repairs. To give them time to do this safely, the estimated time frame to restore your power is now between 22:00 and 23:00.

We're sorry this is taking longer than expected, and we'll continue to keep you up to date, but if any change to this time comes in from our staff I'll let you know as soon as we hear.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 16:26 - Thanks for bearing with us over today. Our team of cable jointers have now identified the suspected section of faulted underground cabling using their specialist equipment.

Our digging team have arrived on site and will now begin excavations, and this can take some time depending on the surface material, and how much space is needed to allow our engineers to assess the fault and make their repairs safely. 

Our estimated time frame of 18:00-19:00 doesn't need to change currently. We'll continue to keep you updated, but our team are always here if you have any questions.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Kevin at 12:49 - We can now confirm that all power in the Auden Place area should be back on.

Our staff are trying to find the root cause and fix it permanently, however with this kind of issue it's likely that power will go off multiple times before we're able to find and fix it. For now we have restored your power as quickly as we can.

If your power isn't on or you would like to talk to us, call us for free on 08003163105. We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our staff have now arrived on site in the Auden Place area. They will start running tests at our substation to see how the local network is operating.

It's quite common for power to be restored briefly and go off again; this is our engineers using testing equipment to energise different sections of our network - this helps to narrow down any possible issues.

Our cabling network is often broken down by smaller sites, and our staff may also check these to test for any problems, and they may also walk the cabling route to see if there are any signs of malfunction that have appeared on the surface. Our time frame hasn't been adjusted, so for now I'll let you know more news as soon as our staff update us.

Sent by Kevin at 12:12.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Kevin at 11:04 - Our staff are on the way to the Auden Place area, and our estimated time frame to have power back on is currently between 14:30 and 15:30. However, this could change once our staff have assessed the local network.

We believe the power cuts you have been experiencing are caused by a fault with one of the cables. Developing faults can start very small, and so can be hard to detect but each power cut usually makes it easier to see, which means our specialist staff can detect it, and work on the repair to prevent further outages.

I'll let you know once our teams arrive.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Decasta at 17:48 - Our staff have now managed to restore power back to the High Road HA9 7 area again.

They have thoroughly investigated our network equipment in the area, but the underlying network fault has not yet developed to a point for it to appear on any of their diagnostic tests. As such, they have replaced our substation equipment to restore power as quickly as possible.

I'm sorry this affected your power again today. This may happen again before we're able to pinpoint the issue and fix it, but if you'd like to talk to us about anything or if your power isn't on, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Decasta at 18:12 - We're sorry your power has gone off again. We are sending our engineers back to the Sycamore Road LU5 5 area to investigate why.

Currently, our time frame for power to be restored is between 21:30-22:30, but once our staff arrive on site and assess the situation, this time may change. As there have been other power cuts recently it's difficult to know what repairs will be needed to restore your power safely, so time frames we provide throughout have to be estimated.

The results of our engineer's tests on our network will let us know if our estimate needs to be adjusted, so we'll update you as soon as they have arrived on site and investigated.
